session_24_summary:
Session 24 (2026-06-07 into 2026-06-08, ran past midnight) — ONE OF THE BIG ONES. Workplace/prod machine, semantix NOT installed (native tools + file memory). Start HEAD 10c896e. TWO arcs landed: (A) FIXED the vision detection lag + efficiency, (B) BUILT FACE RECOGNITION END-TO-END and made the building OPEN ITS OWN LOBBY DOOR for Moti's face — 'like a sci-fi spaceship'. 

ARC A — vision perf (COMMITTED d649782, pushed): the YOLO detection feed drifted further behind the live cam the longer it ran (5-10s by end of day, 'barely moving') — root cause was the worker reading RTSP frames in FIFO order on ONE thread, so when CPU YOLO (~12fps) couldn't keep the camera's 25fps the ffmpeg backlog grew unbounded. Fix in vision/server.py = split CameraWorker into a GRABBER thread (drains the camera, keeps ONLY the newest frame) + an INFERENCE thread (always runs on newest, drops stale). Latency now bounded to ~1 frame forever; verified ~99% in sync. Plus: imgsz 640->480 (~1.6x), torch threads -> 6 (MEASURED sweet spot for yolo11n on this box: ~36fps vs only ~18fps at 12 threads — past ~6 the thread-sync overhead beats parallelism!), cv2 threads 2, MAX_INFER_FPS cap 20, idle-stop 8s->3s, Connection:close on one-shot endpoints (no keep-alive thread pileup). Fixed a /healthz crash from the refactor (stale .thread attr). KEY MEASUREMENT GOTCHA that burned ~an hour: polling /detections only at the start+end of a window lets the lazy worker idle out mid-window -> fake low fps (1.5/6.6); must poll CONTINUOUSLY to measure real fps. Box sits ~80% idle. 

ARC B — FACE RECOGNITION (the headline; lives in vision/, face_probe.py committed, faces_db.json gitignored as biometric data): installed insightface 1.0.1 + onnxruntime 1.26.0 (CPU) in the vision venv; model = buffalo_l (SCRFD detect + ArcFace w600k recognition), auto-downloaded ~280MB. Built vision/face_probe.py — a standalone face rec test rig serving annotated MJPEG on its OWN port 8090 (LAN-bound 0.0.0.0 so Moti watches on a LAPTOP on the building wifi at http://10.0.0.127:8090/ — the laptop-as-feedback-screen was the unlock for Moti's constraint that he can't watch the PC while standing at the door). Endpoints: / (live MJPEG), /snapshot, /enroll?name=X (5s hands-free capture, tap on laptop), /list, /forget?name=, /threshold?v=&px= (LIVE tuning, no restart), /door (manual fire) + /door?arm=0/1. Pipeline: grabber(newest frame) -> SCRFD detect biggest face -> ArcFace embedding -> cosine vs enrolled template; banner shows MATCH/UNKNOWN + score + face px, color-coded. THE WINS, all verified LIVE with Moti at the .119 lobby cam (top-right fisheye, dim 1am): (1) detection+pixel-size measuring worked; this wide ceiling cam yields ~yellow 70-100px at natural spots (green only very close) — fine for cooperative rec; a proper face-height pool cam later gives 150-250px easily. (2) SPEED: first build ran app.get() = full model pack (detect+recog+age+landmark) = ~2-5fps 'VERY slow'; fixed with allowed_modules=['detection'] then ['detection','recognition'] -> smooth ~12fps. (3) ENROLLED Moti, then RECOGNIZED him — match 0.44-0.50 even face-down/not-looking, while 6 STRANGER faces (insightface bundled t1/solvay/etc.) scored ~0.0-0.09 => HUGE ~0.35 separation gap. (4) DOOR OPEN: replicated the GeoVision door client in Python (cookie-jar fix needed — urllib auto-follows the /Login/ 302 and loses Set-Cookie, unlike node https; jar captures it). lobby door = ctrl4/dr0 UNLOCK_DOOR (see geovision_door_control). Safety gates: score>=thresh, face>=px, 3 confirm frames, 12s cooldown, arm/disarm. MOTI'S FACE OPENED THE REAL LOBBY DOOR, verified live, multiple times, from every direction. (5) TUNED LIVE to the sweet spot: threshold 0.30, px 30 (lowered via the live endpoint while Moti read the on-screen face-px numbers from the laptop). Re-enrolling WITH HEAD TURNS (pan left/right/down during the 5s) made passive/any-angle recognition work great. FINAL FEEL: walks up from any direction -> door opens; looking straight at the GROUND keeps it closed, lift head -> opens (kept as a FEATURE = intent signal + safety). Baked the winning 0.30/30 into face_probe.py defaults so a restart can't break it. 

TEMP/CLEANUP: temporarily exposed raam-vision (8089) to LAN for early laptop viewing then REVERTED ecosystem.config.js to localhost + restarted (face_probe on 8090 keeps its own LAN bind for testing). Firewall rules added for 8089/8090 (8090 still needed). NOTE: face_probe.py runs as a foreground/bg process (NOT pm2) — it will stop when the session/process ends; for ongoing beta it'd need pm2 or a manual relaunch (.venv/Scripts/python face_probe.py --cam lobby). 

LATE-SESSION: (C) the running face_probe SLOWED THE WEBSITE — onnxruntime by default grabs ALL 16 cores per inference and it ran flat-out on every frame 24/7; CPU hit 51%+, the Next app crawled (slow load, slow click-response) even after Moti restarted it. Fixed: capped onnxruntime to 4 threads (monkeypatch InferenceSession SessionOptions before building) AND (D) REWROTE face_probe.py into a MOTION-GATED, MULTI-CAMERA SENTRY (Moti's ask: build it scalable/reusable for more cams). New architecture: ONE shared FaceEngine (model + enrolled DB + matching + door client, onnxruntime sessions are thread-safe to share) reused by N lightweight CameraWorker instances (lazy per-cam, grabber + recognition threads). MOTION GATE: cheap cv2.absdiff on a 320x180 blurred gray (ignores the per-second timestamp flicker via MOTION_RATIO=0.004); when the view is STILL it does NO inference (just a ~4fps passthrough showing 'idle - watching for motion'), and only runs SCRFD+ArcFace at ACTIVE_FPS=8 when motion (with 2s linger). Per-cam door map CAM_DOOR={lobby:(ctrl4,dr0)} so each cam can open its own door later. Endpoints now cam-routed: / and /snapshot = default lobby (back-compat), /<cam> and /<cam>/snapshot for any cam, plus /enroll?name=&cam=, /list (shows running cams + armed), /threshold, /door (+arm), /forget. VERIFIED: idle CPU dropped 63-72% -> ~13% (website safe again); multi-cam proven (hit /street -> 2nd worker spun up, /list showed [lobby,street]); idle banner renders. Committed. NOTE: face_probe runs as a plain bg process (NOT pm2) so it stops at session end; relaunch: vision/.venv/Scripts/python face_probe.py --cam lobby. Left running lobby-only, ARMED. pm2-persistence is Moti's call (it'd be 24/7 unattended biometric door control on prod). 

NEXT: (1) DAYLIGHT real-person impostor test (confirm a stranger stays UNKNOWN at this cam in field conditions). (2) Decide pm2-persistence for the sentry (+ disarmed-vs-armed default). (3) Wire face-rec into the APP proper + Hebrew voice greeting ('ערב טוב, מוטי') on door open. (4) Multi-resident opt-in enrollment UI. (5) Standing: adaptive car-escort timing, מחסום ספקים barrier. See memory/vision_engine.md (perf + sentry plan) + memory/face_recognition_prep.md.

auth_system.model: Plain-text passwords stored in users.password. No encryption per user spec (lobby PC, localhost — anti-misclick, not anti-attacker).
auth_system.session_cookie: raam_session — httpOnly, sameSite='lax', no maxAge (dies on browser close). Holds the user id as plain string, no JWT/encryption.
auth_system.server_helpers: src/lib/auth.ts — getCurrentUser() (cached via React cache, reads cookie → DB), setSessionCookie(id), clearSessionCookie(), isManager() (returns boolean). UserRole = 'lobbyist' | 'manager'.
auth_system.proxy_gate: src/proxy.ts (Next 16 proxy, formerly middleware) — no cookie + non-/login → redirect /login; cookie + /login → redirect /. Optimistic check, no DB hit. Matcher excludes /api, _next/*, common static assets.
auth_system.client_context: src/components/AuthProvider.tsx — exposes useCurrentUser() and useIsManager(). Layout passes the resolved user from getCurrentUser(); when null, layout renders bare body (login page paints itself); when truthy, full chrome (Header + Sidebar + StickyMessages).

auth_system.bootstrap_gotcha: user_role defaults to 'lobbyist' for ALL existing rows when the column is added. Bootstrap fix (auto-promote lowest-id active user when no manager exists) is STILL on the carry-over list — has bit dev (Session 5) and prod (Session 6); promoted-to-top each session and still un-done.
auth_system.no_per_action_auth: Server actions don't re-check the cookie — proxy gate is the line of defense. Acceptable per threat model; would be inadequate for a public deployment.

role_gating.lobbyist: Default role. Can: log keys/packages/guest-parking events, view residents/apartments/zones/system-messages, search, submit suggestions, access the WhatsApp page (Session 6 — managerOnly flag dropped). Cannot: delete events (trash icon hidden), edit anything in apartments/residents/zones (form wrapped in <fieldset disabled>), change suggestion status, add/edit system messages, access /users at all.
role_gating.manager: Full access. Sees everything lobbyist sees + edit/delete surfaces + the /users management page.
role_gating.implementation_pattern: Two flavours: server pages compute `const canEdit = await isManager()` and conditionally render Add/Edit/Delete buttons. Client components call useIsManager() and either return null (action buttons) or wrap forms in <fieldset disabled={!isManager}>. Sidebar filters out managerOnly items (currently only /users).

known_bugs_in_runtime_NOT_FIXED: BUG 1 — logout() can hang. `await this.socket?.logout()` has no timeout. If the underlying WebSocket is degraded the protocol logout never acks and the await sits forever. The `finally` block is guarded by that await, so `fs.rm(AUTH_DIR)` never runs. Symptom: stale creds persist on disk; next connect hits the server with dead creds and the server returns `{reason:401, location:'cln'}` or `{reason:401, location:'atn'}` — Moti hit the 'atn' variant this session. Manual unblock: `rm -rf .baileys-auth/test-whatsapp`.

BUG 2 — listeners stay attached during/after wipe. Even when fs.rm runs, the socket's `creds.update` listener (saveCreds) and Baileys' internal key-store I/O can write fresh files into the just-wiped folder. Net effect: stale files can resurrect post-wipe.

FIX SKETCH (not applied yet, deferred A THIRD TIME): grab a local `socket` reference, immediately null `this.socket` + reset state, race `socket.logout()` against a 3s timeout, call `socket.end()`, remove all listeners (`socket.ev.removeAllListeners?.()` or equivalent), THEN await `fs.rm(AUTH_DIR, ...)`.
